__about: Locked UX baseline for IndustryOS Control Center cockpits. Sprint 13.5 PR #4 onwards. Sibling research memo: ../../../../../../Library/Application Support/Claude/.../memory/research_luxury_multivertical_cockpit_ux.md
name: IndustryOS Tokens v2 (Luxury Cockpit Baseline)

typography.__notes: Inter for UI, JetBrains Mono for numerics/IDs. Geist-derived 14-size scale. Negative tracking on every heading >= 24px is the single biggest 'expensive' tell.

fontFamily.mono: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, monospace
fontFamily.__notes: Two families only. Adding a third is a rejected anti-pattern.

fontFeatureSettings.__notes: tnum is required on every DataTable mono cell + KPI numeric. cv11 gives Inter the disambiguated single-story 'a' that reads more professional.

label-14.__usage: Most-common UI text (queue row, button, label)

spacing.__notes: 4-px base with asymmetric values (6, 12, 20, 40, 48, 96). Asymmetric values are the luxury tell — they show the spacing was chosen, not derived from a rigid grid.

motion.__notes: All animations <= 400ms. ONE enter ease (cubic-bezier 0.2 0 0 1) and ONE exit ease (cubic-bezier 0.4 0 1 1). No springs, no overshoot, no bounce. Cockpit surfaces feel glassy, not playful.

easing.__notes: Linear is RESERVED for skeleton shimmer and rotations only. Never apply to translate or opacity transitions.

reducedMotion.__notes: When prefers-reduced-motion is set, halve all durations and disable translate/scale; opacity-only transitions remain.

elevation.__notes: Three tiers only. Heavy shadows kill the luxury read instantly. Borders + flat surfaces beat shadows.

radius.__notes: Vercel ships 0-4 across entire system. We allow up to 16 for the Cherry Bar modal, but default sit at 4-6.
radius.__usage: 1: hairline chip. 2: button. 3: chip / pill / card corner. 4: input. 5: drawer. 6: Cherry Bar modal. full: user avatar ONLY.

density.__notes: The cockpit picks. Users never see a toggle in v1. Three modes: comfortable (detail), compact (queue), dense (DataTable).

comfortable.__usage: Detail panes, preview pane, card body

color.__notes: Monochrome chassis + ONE accent (CherryAI red) + tri-state semantic. Source-of-truth for hex values is tokens.css v3.0; this file references the variable names and the chosen role mapping.

discipline.rule: Color is for meaning, not decoration (Stripe rule).
discipline.accent: CherryAI red #cf3339, used SPARINGLY (primary CTA, focus ring, brand mark).
discipline.semantic: Tri-state ONLY: success (green), warning (amber), danger (red). No 'info-blue'. No 'purple-AI'.
discipline.vertical: Vertical signal lives in the chip, never the page chrome. Monochrome regardless of vertical.

surface.__notes: Three surface tiers max. Stop here.

border.__notes: 1 is default; 2 is emphasis; 3 is subtle/recess.

text.__notes: 1 primary, 2 secondary, 3 tertiary/metadata, 4 disabled/muted. AI-generated content uses text-2 plus a glyph, never a unique color.

accent.__notes: Accent rendered as 2-px solid focus ring. Never as default border. Never on inactive states.

verticalChip.__notes: The single primitive for vertical overlays. Renders ONLY when entity has the signal; never reserves space when empty.

shape.__notes: One color always. Severity overrides text and ring only; background stays neutral.

dataTable.__notes: Chip shape does not survive in dense table view.

invisibility.rule: If the entity has no vertical signal, the chip slot is completely absent. No whitespace reservation. No placeholder.

tenantModeBand.__notes: The ONE banner pattern. Reserved for 'you are not in production data'. Vertical signals never use a banner. Project context never uses a banner.

cherryBar.__notes: The single voice + Cmd-K entry point. Lives top-right in header (NOT floating FAB, NOT side panel). Cmd-K opens from anywhere. Cmd-Shift-V opens with voice already active.

modal.__notes: Centered modal at top of viewport. NOT full-screen. NOT side panel.

transcription.__notes: Granola pattern: interim transcript in muted gray, final transcript in primary text.

narration.__notes: ADR-014 D6: state-mutating commands ALWAYS require user confirmation; TTS never auto-plays.

voiceActiveIndicator.viewportHairline: 2px accent gradient at top of viewport, gentle pulse with audio amplitude

focusRing.__notes: 2px solid accent. NEVER box-shadow (Tailwind default is the same on every site on earth).

datatable.__notes: Spreadsheet-density view. Dense rows, tabular numerics, hairline borders.
datatable.__notes2: No striped rows. Stripes read consumer-app.

skeleton.__notes: Slow pulse only. Fast pulse reads cheap.

antiPatterns.__notes: Inlined here so the JSON can be linted in CI for direct violations. Add a Stylelint / custom rule to fail PRs that introduce these.
rejected: Material-style heavy box shadows on cards
Springs with overshoot/bounce on cockpit surfaces (M3 Expressive default)
Rainbow status badges (only green/amber/red)
Per-vertical theme palettes
Multiple font families beyond Inter + JetBrains Mono
Animations longer than 400ms
Default Tailwind tracking on display headings (>=24px must have -0.04em)
More than 3 surface elevation tiers
Tooltip on every cell
User-toggleable density control in UI chrome (v1)
Force-onboarding wizards
Sample data on empty states
Sidebar nav items visible to tenants who can't use the feature
Vertical chip slots that reserve whitespace when empty
Banner stacks (more than one full-width banner at a time)
Floating mic FAB at bottom-right
Sidebar AI pane that always takes 320px
Voice trigger only on cockpit pages
TTS auto-narration without user gesture
Two separate command palettes (one Cmd-K text, one Cmd-Shift-V voice)
AI-generated content colored with a 'purple is AI' convention
Coloring the page based on vertical
Showing a vertical's chrome to a tenant who doesn't use that vertical
